content:
- Completed story-12 task 03 execution. Added typed source-command auditing in `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_integrity.rs`, routed post-setup source SQL through the wrapper-backed audited path, and exposed typed post-setup audit access from the default bootstrap harness.
- Strengthened the E2E integrity contract and the default long-lane live-update scenario so the suite now proves destination catch-up after setup happens without extra source bootstrap/admin/helper commands. Also fit composite and multi-mapping support to the shared audit owner and replaced fresh-per-harness Docker networks with a shared serialized E2E network to keep `make test-long` stable.
- All required lanes passed: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`. Updated the task file to completed with `<passes>true</passes>`, checked off the plan, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and the next step is to commit and push all repo plus `.ralph` changes.

- Refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, then checked chooser rules, active tasks, completed tasks, README, and the story 13 candidate task files.
- Confirmed there are no unblocked failing bug tasks, no visible blocker tags on active tasks, and no meta-task eligible because several unblocked failing tasks remain.
- Chose story 13 task 01 as the next task because the real codebase is a Rust workspace with an existing README contract surface and this is the earliest unblocked failing verification task for the public novice-user path; next step is to write that path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill files.
- Confirmed the active task is `story-13-verify-novice-user/01-task-verify-readme-alone-is-sufficient-for-novice-user.md`; next step is to inspect that task file and current git state to determine whether I must plan or execute.
- If the task or plan still says `TO BE VERIFIED`, I need to create or refine a plan in the task plan subdir and stop immediately after switching it to the correct state.

- Read the active task body and confirmed it is still `not_started` with `<passes>false</passes>` and no linked plan, so this turn should stay in planning mode.
- Checked git status; only Ralph bookkeeping files are dirty right now, with `.ralph/current_task.txt` untracked.
- Next I am reading the README, story task directory, and relevant tests to build a TDD execution plan that also calls out one boundary cleanup opportunity.

- Read the top-level README and confirmed the quick-start path is a long Docker-centric sequence spanning validate, schema compare, postgres setup, helper plan, and runtime startup.
- Found an existing `crates/runner/tests/readme_contract.rs`; next I am checking whether it only enforces string presence or already models a novice README-only journey.
- I also need one concrete boundary smell to target during execution so the implementation can flatten test/documentation verification instead of introducing another ad hoc layer.

- Checked adjacent story-13 task files to keep scope clean: task 02 owns direct Docker/no-wrapper verification, task 03 owns copyable config and quick-start clarity.
- Identified the likely task-01 gap: the README quick start references schema export artifacts without README-owned instructions for producing them, which means a novice may still need repo lore or source inspection.
- Next I am confirming existing public command/artifact contracts so the plan can specify README-only closure checks instead of vague documentation assertions.

- Created the story-13 task-01 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-13-verify-novice-user/01-task-verify-readme-alone-is-sufficient-for-novice-user_plans/2026-04-19-readme-novice-user-plan.md` and linked it from the task file with a new `<plan>` tag.
- The plan is ready for execution and ends with `NOW EXECUTE`; it scopes task 01 to README-only path closure, identifies the current schema-export gap, and sets the improve-code-boundaries target as moving README parsing/assertion logic into a dedicated docs-contract support module.
- Next turn should read the task file plus this plan, then execute strictly by TDD vertical slices, finish with `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`, and only then update acceptance boxes and `<passes>true</passes>`.

- Read previous Ralph progress, confirmed the active task path, and reopened the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions for this turn.
- Confirmed the last turn already produced an execution-ready story-13 task-01 plan, so this turn is executing rather than replanning.
- Next I am reading the task file and linked plan, then I will implement the README novice-user closure work in red-green TDD slices and keep appending progress during execution.

- Read the task file and execution plan. Confirmed this turn should execute the plan because it ends with `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Inspected `README.md`, `crates/runner/tests/readme_contract.rs`, and nearby CLI/artifact contracts. The concrete README-only novice gap is still the schema export prerequisite: the README references `/schema/crdb_schema.txt` and `/schema/pg_schema.sql` without README-owned instructions for producing them.
- Next I am adding a typed README contract support module and the first failing red test around that schema-artifact closure gap, then I will make the minimum README change to satisfy it.

- Executed three red-green README-contract slices so far: explicit schema export commands for compare/helper-plan inputs, explicit `bash cockroach-bootstrap.sh` execution, and explicit instruction to run generated `grants.sql` before runtime start.
- Added a typed README support module at `crates/runner/tests/support/readme_contract.rs` so README loading, section slicing, and ordered phrase checks stop living directly in the behavior test file.
- Added one more red-green contract to make the quick start explicitly state that it does not require reading repo internals such as `crates/`, `tests/`, or `investigations/`. Next I am running the full `readme_contract` suite and then the repo-wide required lanes.

- Finished all required verification lanes for story-13 task-01: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed cleanly.
- The implementation stayed documentation-contract focused: README quick start now explicitly covers schema export generation, manual bootstrap script execution, grant application before runtime, and an explicit no-repo-internals promise. The README behavior tests now route through a dedicated support boundary.
- Next I am updating the task file plus execution plan checkboxes, setting `<passes>true</passes>`, running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then committing and pushing all repo plus Ralph bookkeeping changes.
